name: DOGE State Change Momentum Hold v1
edge_mechanism: state-change momentum after regime improvement and post-break hold
thesis: DOGE 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing whether a fresh state-change move can continue after a break and hold above fast value, even in a market that is mostly chop with a small transitional component. The edge is not raw breakout chasing; it is participation only after the market proves that the new directional state is being accepted.
invalidation_condition: Abort if post-break holds fail to extend, if the setup is too sparse, or if state-change signals collapse into one-bar fakeouts.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price improves from chop into a fresh directional phase, holds above fast value after the break, and then delivers a second-leg continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In transition regimes, many breaks fail immediately, but a small subset establishes a new directional state. Waiting for the hold should filter out some false starts while still capturing the first continuation leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is appropriate because regime shifts are expressive enough to matter while remaining slow enough to manage with bar-based rules.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market remains mostly rotational, the hold will occur but momentum will fail to produce a real second leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are valid but extension is weak, adjust exit ambition and time-based de-risking before touching the hold logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A reasonable number of fresh-momentum entries with first-target capture doing most expectancy and occasional runner contribution.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: DOGE 4h and one cross-lane show credible density and positive post-cost continuation after the hold.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or repeated hold entries that fail to accelerate.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; break, hold, and state confirmation are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad hold logic rather than rare breakout conditions

validation_basket.why_included: Best expressive lane for regime-improvement momentum in the active basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Hold entries show credible density and positive post-cost second-leg travel.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Hold entries are too sparse or repeatedly fail to accelerate.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fresh momentum breaks that hold and deliver a second 4h leg.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism is structural across a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows comparable state-change momentum behavior.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner but sometimes smaller second-leg continuation than DOGE.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same state-change logic survives on a more violent expressive lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO preserves the mechanism without collapsing into noise.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO becomes chaotic and invalidates the hold filter.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Patchier but larger second-leg continuation than DOGE.

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.14 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.14 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
